input.background:
$\mathrm{NO}_2$ concentrations (in parts per billion) in the exhaust fumes of different types of vehicle (cars, minibuses and buses) were sampled and the following data were obtained:

\begin{tabular}{lll}
\hline Car & Minibus & Bus \\
\hline 69 & 51 & 41 \\
62 & 45 & 48 \\
73 & 59 & 42
\end{tabular}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Car & Minibus & Bus \\
\hline 70 & 57 & 39 \\
\hline 68 & 58 & 47 \\
\hline 68 & 54 & 49 \\
\hline 64 & 46 & 35 \\
\hline 71 & 48 & 41 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: Check that the variances of the three samples are not significantly different using an $F_{\text {max }}$ test.

output.answer:
The highest variance is for minibuses (30.786).

The lowest variance is for cars (12.982).
$F_{	ext{max}}=2.37, k=3, 	ext{df}=7, P>0.05$; therefore the variances are not significantly different.

meta_info.chapter: Differences between more than two samples
meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
